title: Site Fragmentation
body: In the geographic distribution of clinical research, does the fragmentation of trial sites relative to participant volume indicate a structural divide in research infrastructure between Africa and Europe? This study evaluated site-to-enrollment ratios for 800 trials using the location-module and design-module metadata from the ClinicalTrials.gov API v2 database system. Investigators applied a fragmentation model and reported the sites-per-thousand participants as the primary comparative estimand for research density and institutional integration. The primary result showed that Africa exhibits a very low fragmentation index of thirteen, compared to nearly two hundred in European research networks during the audit. This indicates that African research is concentrated in high-volume mega-sites, whereas European research is distributed across a dense grid of smaller integrated centers. These findings highlight a structural divide where Africa provides massive enrollment from few nodes while Europe maintains a resilient multi-node innovation ecosystem. The results are limited by the underreporting of specific site locations in some global hubs now. - DOI: 10.156/site-fragmentation - Date: 2026-03-28 - Type: research - Mode: efficiency
ai_transparency: LLM assistance was used for drafting and language editing. The author reviewed and edited the manuscript and takes responsibility for the final content.
